funding.rawTextSnippet: Tokenmaxxing was the hottest trend in Silicon Valley earlier this year, with CEOs encouraging employees to push AI usage as far as it would go. Then the bill came due. Uber reportedly blew through its annual AI budget in a few months, some companies cut Claude licenses …

dailyBrief: In a surprising move, AMD has quietly removed memory encryption from its consumer Ryzen CPUs, raising concerns about data security for businesses relying on these processors. Meanwhile, a team has developed a persistent agent memory layer on Elasticsearch, achieving a recall rate of 0.89, which could enhance data retrieval and operational efficiency for enterprises. On a more alarming note, leaked financial documents reveal that OpenAI is incurring losses of billions annually, prompting questions about the sustainability of its business model. Additionally, Anthropic has dispatched a hacker to address government concerns regarding AI safety, highlighting the increasing scrutiny on AI technologies. In innovation news, the launch of Adam, an open-source AI CAD tool from Y Combinator's Winter 2025 batch, could disrupt the design industry by making advanced design capabilities more accessible to startups and smaller firms.
